channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️🛢️ Ukraine Strikes Primorsk Port and Shadow Fleet Tankers in Major Drone Offensive
item.description: Ukraine launched drone attacks on Russia's Baltic Sea oil port of Primorsk, setting facilities ablaze and striking multiple vessels. The strikes represent an escalation in Ukraine's campaign against Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: ☢️🇺🇦🚨 IAEA Reports Drone Strike Near Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Radiation Lab
item.description: A drone targeted the external radiation control laboratory at the Russian-held Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in southeastern Ukraine. The IAEA said the incident raised concerns about nuclear safety near the facility.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇫🇮🛸 Ukraine Offers Finland Drone Technology Deal, Zelenskiy Announces
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskiy proposed strengthening Ukraine-Finland partnership through a drone technology sharing agreement, leveraging combat expertise developed during the war with Russia.

item.title: 🇪🇺📉💰 ECB Official: Euro Zone Recession Fears Are Real and Justified Amid Iran War
item.description: Bank of Greece governor Yannis Stournaras warned that euro zone recession concerns stemming from the Middle East conflict are "real and justified." He emphasized that Iran war negotiations will be critical for ECB monetary policy decisions.

item.title: 🇮🇱✈️💰 Israel Approves Purchase of Two New F-35 and F-15IA Fighter Squadrons
item.description: Israel gave final approval for purchasing two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA fighter aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing in a deal worth tens of billions of dollars, the defense ministry announced.

item.title: 🏦 Fed Governor Barr warns private credit stress could trigger psychological contagion
item.description: Fed Governor Michael Barr warned that stress in private credit markets could spark psychological contagion leading to broader credit crunch, Bloomberg News reported.

item.title: 💰 Kashkari: Iran war limits Fed ability to provide rate guidance
item.description: Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari said the ongoing Iran war increases risks of higher inflation and economic damage, limiting how much guidance the central bank should provide on rate policy.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran executes man convicted for killing security officer during 2022 unrest
item.description: Iran executed a man convicted of involvement in the killing of a security officer during the 2022 protests, the judiciary news outlet Mizan reported.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea launches next-generation Earth observation satellite on SpaceX Falcon 9
item.description: South Korea launched its next-generation mid-sized Earth observation satellite No. 2 aboard SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California.

item.title: 🏛️ Supreme Court Justice Gorsuch calls for candid conversations amid leak concerns
item.description: Justice Neil Gorsuch expressed unease about continuing leaks of internal deliberations at the Supreme Court, citing the need for justices to engage in candid conversations.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israel orders evacuations of 11 towns in southern Lebanon amid Hezbollah operations
item.description: The Israeli military issued urgent evacuation warnings to residents of 11 towns and villages in southern Lebanon, telling them to move at least 1,000 meters away. The order comes as Israel continues operations against Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Two US troops missing during African Lion military exercise in Morocco
item.description: Two US service members participating in the African Lion joint military exercises were reported missing near Tan Tan in southern Morocco, with search operations underway.

item.title: 🇦🇲 Zelenskyy visit to Armenia signals shift away from Russian influence
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy is expected to visit Yerevan for the European Political Community summit, marking a significant diplomatic shift as former Soviet states distance themselves from Russia.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Morgan Stanley predicts B investment surge in India amid West Asia crisis
item.description: Geopolitical instability in West Asia is accelerating India push toward domestic manufacturing and defense indigenization, with Morgan Stanley forecasting an billion investment wave over five years.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel approves purchase of two new fighter squadrons
item.description: The Israeli government has approved the purchase of two new fighter squadrons to secure air superiority in the coming decade, drawing lessons from the recent confrontation with Iran.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC+ set for third oil output hike since Hormuz closure, sources say
item.description: OPEC+ is expected to agree to another increase in oil production quotas, the third since Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz. However, the increase will remain largely on paper as long as Gulf oil supplies remain disrupted.

item.title: 🇺🇸 CBS News: Trump reviewing Iran peace proposal as congressional deadline passes
item.description: President Trump is reviewing a new 14-point peace proposal from Iran aimed at ending the conflict. The deadline for congressional authorization under the War Powers Resolution has passed, raising questions about the legal basis for continued US involvement.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi in Critical Condition in Iranian Hospital
item.description: Jailed Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi remains in critical condition in a cardiac care unit in northwestern Iran after being transferred from prison. Her family foundation reports her health is deteriorating due to her continued imprisonment.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Four Injured in South London Shooting
item.description: Four people were hospitalized with gunshot wounds in a shooting in south London. Police are investigating whether the incident is related to a stabbing that occurred the same day.

item.title: 🇪🇺 ECB Policymaker Warns Euro Zone Recession Fears Are Real
item.description: Bank of Greece governor Yannis Stournaras warned that euro zone recession concerns are "real and justified" as Middle East conflict continues. He emphasized that talks to end the Iran war will be critical for ECB monetary policy decisions.

item.title: 🌏 Asian Development Bank Launches Critical Minerals Finance Scheme
item.description: The ADB launched a new financing initiative to help Asia-Pacific countries develop supply chains for critical minerals used in clean energy, batteries, and electric vehicles. The move addresses growing demand for strategic resources.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Pays Steep Price in Battle to Reverse Fortunes
item.description: Israel has displaced hundreds of thousands of Hezbollah constituents and killed as many as several thousand of its fighters. Domestic rivals are hardening opposition against Hezbollah status as an armed group.

item.title: 🇯🇵 SoftBank to develop lithium-free batteries for Japanese data centers
item.description: Japan's SoftBank Group plans to manufacture batteries without expensive lithium and cobalt, aiming to power the country's growing data center infrastructure while reducing dependence on critical minerals.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US congressional Republicans question prosecution of former FBI Director Comey
item.description: Republican Senator Thom Tillis expressed reservations about the prosecution of former FBI Director James Comey, signaling potential intra-party tensions over the Justice Department's approach.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden seizes Russian shadow fleet tanker in Baltic Sea
item.description: The Swedish Coast Guard seized a tanker believed to be part of Russia's shadow fleet operating in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a series of similar actions targeting vessels circumventing sanctions.
